measurement_note: Size is n, the digit-string length. Each workload is a repeated-nine string with no valid split, so the reference considers every possible first boundary and scans the unmatched suffix, taking O(n^2) time and O(n) auxiliary digit storage. A correct slower implementation that enumerates every boundary subset tests all 2^(n-1) partitions, completes all legal tiers through the source maximum n = 20, and fails only by scaling.

cases.name: benchmark: unsplittable repeated-nine string of length 4

benchmark: unsplittable repeated-nine string of length 20
